summary:
HIV-infected patients with end-stage renal disease should not be universally excluded from transplantation because safe antiretroviral therapies exist that prevent virologic failure while minimizing nephrotoxic effects. Well-managed HIV under suppressive treatment does not constitute an absolute barrier to organ transplantation according to established medical guidelines.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$. Answer choice: B
